title: Services, Units, Boot State, and Logs · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Diagnose a service that is enabled but unavailable by separating desired boot state, current process state, dependencies, configuration, and logs. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Relate service managers, units, dependencies, targets, process supervision, and application readiness.
Use status, is-active, is-enabled, show, list-dependencies, and failed-unit evidence before changing service state.
Query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 2. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

Inspect the Service Unit Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate service managers, units, dependencies, targets, process supervision, and application readiness.

content.text: For Service Unit Model, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Service Unit Model precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 2. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: An enabled service is necessarily running and healthy now.

stimulus.title: Service Unit Model case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 2. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: An enabled service is necessarily running and healthy now.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate service managers, units, dependencies, targets, process supervision, and application readiness. Artifact under review: a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Service Unit Model, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Service Recovery · fault isolation
steps.instruction:
debug case — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 2. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

Which observation would disprove this suspected cause: “Automatic infinite restart is a complete availability strategy.”?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define restart limits, readiness checks, dependency order, failure escalation, and rollback evidence for a service change.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe Linux operations investigation headed "# Evidence: linux-service-recovery-s4sq62" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Service Recovery into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: linux-service-recovery-s4sq62
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 3. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Service Recovery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Service Recovery assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 4. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

content.title: Ordering constraint

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Service Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define restart limits, readiness checks, dependency order, failure escalation, and rollback evidence for a service change.

hints: Name the Service Recovery precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Automatic infinite restart is a complete availability strategy.
